summary: Stellar Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain & soaring Sugaree
blurb: A tight first set builds into a powerhouse second‑set suite (Bertha → Estimated Prophet → Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain → Saint Stephen). Jerry’s solos on Sugaree, Peggy‑O and Looks Like Rain are highlights, with Phil’s melodic bass driving the jams. Betty Cantor’s soundboard capture is crystal‑clear and showcases every nuance.
review: May 5 1977 at New Haven’s Veterans Memorial Coliseum captured one of spring ’77’s finest moments. The first set rolls out a relaxed yet precise groove, highlighted by a gorgeous Looks Like Rain solo where Jerry’s note choice is described as “perfect” and Phil’s bass line “incredibly melodic and flowing.” Sugaree then erupts into an epic Garcia showcase that several listeners called “magical,” while Peggy‑O shines with soaring leads. The second set launches from Bertha into a fluid Estimated Prophet, then bursts into the legendary Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain sequence that keeps the energy high before morphing into a fiery Saint Stephen. Keith’s piano/organ adds color on the jams, and the twin drummers lock in tight, thundering rhythms. Across all five soundboard sources the recording quality is excellent—Betty Cantor’s board work is praised as crystal‑clear with only minor dropouts—making this the definitive version to experience the night’s brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of six recordings are explicitly positive, repeatedly praising Jerry’s solos, the Scarlet>Fire segment and overall band tightness; two mixed reviews note only minor issues (track splitting, matrix gaps). The strong consensus yields a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Betty Cantor’s soundboard mix is crystal‑clear, captures the tight first set and the blissful second‑set jams with only negligible dropouts.

key_highlights: Iconic Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain sequence that drives the second set.
Jerry’s soaring solos on Sugaree, Peggy‑O and Looks Like Rain receive unanimous praise.
Phil’s melodic bass lines provide a “boat launch” groove throughout the show.
Betty Cantor’s SBD capture offers excellent clarity, making it the go‑to source.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers perfect note choice on Looks Like Rain, soaring solos on Sugaree, Peggy‑O and Saint Stephen; his phrasing is called magical and epic throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides thumping, melodic bass—highlighted as “incredibly flowing” on Looks Like Rain and a solid foundation for the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Adds strong vocal/guitar interplay; noted as more active than usual, though some wish Sugar Magnolia had been extended.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s piano/organ work shines especially during Estimated Prophet and the Scarlet>Fire jam, despite occasional perfunctory runs elsewhere.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is tight and thundering, driving the high‑energy sections of both sets.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey, delivering a solid rhythmic backbone that propels the improvisations.
